_purpose: The on-screen UI text the scanner keys off. Two jobs:
atlasGate  — proves the Atlas map is open, so we need not run a full-screen OCR every second.
panel*     — identifies the Uncharted Waters tooltip among everything else on screen.
These are INTERFACE strings, not item names: poe2db and the wiki do not carry them, so both locales
were read off screenshots of the running game (2026-07-14). Do not 'improve' them from a dictionary —
a string that does not match byte-for-byte produces a silent no-match, the exact failure R3 exists to
prevent. Match case-insensitively; the game styles these in small-caps and OCR reports casing loosely.

en.panelHint: Use a logbook to chart the area
en._verified: screenshot of the running English client, 2026-07-14

ru.panelHint: Используйте журнал, чтобы нанести область на карту
ru._verified: screenshot of the running Russian client, 2026-07-14 — every string confirmed on screen

_atlasAnchors_note: ANY one of these proves the Atlas is open. Several, OR'd, on purpose — see Q6.
'World'/'Мир' alone is a trap: it is one STYLISED word on a decorated banner, and Windows OCR returns
nothing at all for it at some upscale factors while reading it cleanly at neighbouring ones (measured;
it cost the predecessor an hour). In Russian it is three letters. So it stays in the list, but it is
not allowed to be the only thing standing between us and a working app.
The others are ordinary UI text in an ordinary font, which OCR handles incomparably better. And the
redundancy is not paranoia: in a real screenshot the rumour panel PARTIALLY COVERS the search box — so
that anchor vanishes exactly when the tooltip is open, which is when the app matters most. Occlusion by
the game's own panels is normal, and any single anchor can be the one that is covered.
